title: The Century Report: March 18, 2026
description:
TCR 03/18/26: Safety Becomes a Weapon

The Justice Department filed its response to Anthropic's federal lawsuit yesterday, arguing that the safety commitments embedded in Claude's architecture could constitute a sabotage vector during active military operations - while the Pentagon separately disclosed plans to establish secure environments where AI companies would train models directly on classified military intelligence data, a qualitative shift from current deployments where models analyze classified documents without learning from them. Mamba 3 arrived as an open-source state-space architecture whose benchmarks exceed same-size Transformers by approximately two points on average, with computational costs that scale linearly rather than quadratically as context windows grow - structural diversification of the AI architecture landscape at the moment of greatest Transformer dominance. MIT physicists published the first direct observation of superconducting electrons moving in a frictionless quantum oscillation inside a copper-oxide material, using a terahertz microscope that compressed long-wavelength light into a space small enough to resolve phenomena predicted by theory but never before seen; and Northwestern surgeons kept a patient alive for 48 hours without lungs using an engineered artificial lung system, delivering the first molecular proof that some acute respiratory failure is irreversible and requires transplantation rather than support - a patient now living normally more than two years later.

Read the full edition: https://sharedsapience.com/the-century-report-march-18-2026/
